# AI Chat Reporting — Vars

Var definitions are derived from `module.lowdefy.yaml`. Pass these via the `vars:` block in your app's `lowdefy.yaml` entry for this module.

## Summary

| Name | Type | Default | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `catalog` | object |  | Yes | The collections catalog — the data dictionary AND the confidentiality/authorization boundary. A map keyed by collection name (`{ [collectionName]: { roles, description, fields, relationships } }`); `collection` and every `$lookup.from` in an AI-authored pipeline must be a key of this map, and the engine enforces the union of the touched collections' `roles` (base plus all lookups, recursively). Declaring a collection here is the act of exposing it. Per-entry keys — `roles` (optional string[] of user roles allowed to query this collection; ABSENT or EMPTY means any authenticated user may query it — role-gating is opt-in), `description` (what the collection holds — prompt material for the agent), `fields` (optional map of field name → `{ type: one of string\|number\|boolean\|date\|object\|array, description, values (optional enum values — feeds report select-filter options), and the optional display hints format, currency, locale, decimals — prompt material the agent copies into presentation contracts, never enforcement }`), and `relationships` (optional array of `{ field, collection, foreignField }` declaring which fields join to which collections so the agent can author correct `$lookup`s). Feeds the agent's instructions at build time and is the allowlist every pipeline validates against. To expose a collection while hiding some fields (or to pre-bake array/object-array/cross- collection joins at a fixed grain), declare a read-only MongoDB view as a first-class catalog entry — see the "reporting over complex data" guide.  |
| `reports_collection` | string | `report_layouts` |  | Collection name for saved report specs. |
| `conversations_collection` | string | `conversations` |  | Collection name for chat conversations. |
| `model` | string | `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5` |  | Gateway model id (provider/model) for the reporting assistant, e.g. anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.5 or anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5.  |
